name: the-origins-of-totalitarianism-with-a-new-introduction-by-the-author
description: Hannah Arendt's foundational analysis of Nazism and Stalinism — tracing the roots of totalitarianism through antisemitism, imperialism, and the breakdown of the nation-state.
useCase: Understand how totalitarian movements arise — the roles of antisemitism, imperialism, mass society, isolation, and ideological terror.
knownRisks: Dense academic text — requires careful reading
Some historical details have been updated since publication
Arendt's views on antisemitism have been debated
